title: Berason

{{Quote|Early in the war, the Empire blockaded the world of Berason because of its political views.|Airen Cracken, ''Cracken's Rebel Field Guide''|<ref name="CRFG" />}}
'''Berason''' was a [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Mining/Legends|mining]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] located in the [[Eclorar sector/Legends|Eclorar sector]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Berason—the inhabitants of which [[Politics/Legends|politically]] opposed the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]—was subjected to an [[Blockade of Berason|Imperial blockade]] that cut off the world's sources of [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] and medical supplies. Eventually, a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] force [[Commanding officer/Legends|led]] by [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] ran the [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]], performing a strike against Berason's [[Berason Imperial base|Imperial base]] and paving way for an Alliance [[Convoy/Legends|convoy]] to bring supplies to the world.

==Description==
Berason was a [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="CRFG" /> located in the [[Berason system/Legends|Berason system]], a part of the [[Eclorar sector/Legends|Eclorar sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" />

==History==
{{Quote|I was put in charge of a force to get supplies to the world below—both food and military—and then lead a strike against the world's Imperial base.|Airen Cracken, ''Cracken's Rebel Field Guide''|<ref name="CRFG" />}}
[[File:Defendermine.png|thumb|right|250px|The Galactic Empire used Defender ion mines to blockade Berason.]]
In the early phase of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], due to Berason's inhabitants opposing the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the latter [[Blockade of Berason|blockaded]] the world by seeding its near [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] with [[Mark III Merr-Sonn Defender Ion Mine|Defender ion mines]]. The [[Ion mine/Legends|mines]] disabled any approaching [[Starship/Legends|starships]]. Due to the [[Merchant/Legends|trade]] with other worlds, which was the main source of medical supplies for Berason, being cut off as a result, the Berason [[Homeworld/Legends|natives]] became ill and their resolve to oppose the Empire began to weaken.<ref name="CRFG" />

In response, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] placed [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] in [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] of a force tasked with running the [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] and delivering [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] and military supplies to Berason, followed by a strike against that world's Imperial base. Cracken's force circumvented the ion [[Space mine/Legends|mines]] by sending out [[Gender/Legends|men]] equipped with [[Environment suit/Legends|environmental suits]] and [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpacks]], with the [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] of the explosives not being able to detect anything smaller than four [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in diameter. Although the process was very slow, eventually Cracken's unit succeeded in clearing a path for a massive Alliance [[Convoy/Legends|convoy]] to Berason, and the [[Officer/Legends|officer]] later recorded his experiences at that world in the ''[[Cracken's Rebel Field Guide]]'', a [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] he had [[Author/Legends|composed]] that documented various pieces of equipment used during the Galactic Civil War.<ref name="CRFG" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|Berason is a low tech mining world that depended upon food and medical supplies from other planets in exchange for minerals.|Airen Cracken, ''Cracken's Rebel Field Guide''|<ref name="CRFG" />}}
A low-tech [[Mining/Legends|mining]] world, Berason depended on other planets for food and medical supplies, merchanting them [[Mineral/Legends|minerals]] in return. The inhabitants of Berason were [[Politics/Legends|politically]] opposed to the Galactic Empire, which had established a [[Berason Imperial base|base]] at the world.<ref name="CRFG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Berason was first mentioned in the [[1991]] sourcebook [[Cracken's Rebel Field Guide (sourcebook)|''Cracken's Rebel Field Guide'']], which was written by [[Christopher Kubasik]] for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="CRFG" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Berason [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the world itself, in grid square Q-12.<ref name="TEA" />
